title: coreopsis piurana
intro: Coreopsis piurana, also known as the Drummond's coreopsis, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its vibrant yellow or orange flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, derived from its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Coreopsis piurana are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Coreopsis piurana typically involve infusions or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
